result.markdown:
--- title: Mapping Trips to Aunt Zerviah's --- *"They have all with the exception of Mother Lib and me gone to Aunt Zerviah to spend the evening I tired out every day by dark as I have no place to lie down and rest during the day. Lib and I am going to retire early"* **Celestia Webster's Diary - November 13, 1867** ## Map of Trips to Aunt Zerviah - One line for each individual ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(tidyverse) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ── ✔ dplyr 1.2.0 ✔ readr 2.2.0 ✔ forcats 1.0.1 ✔ stringr 1.6.0 ✔ ggplot2 4.0.2 ✔ tibble 3.3.1 ✔ lubridate 1.9.5 ✔ tidyr 1.3.2 ✔ purrr 1.2.1 ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ── ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter() ✖ dplyr::lag() masks stats::lag() ℹ Use the conflicted package () to force all conflicts to become errors ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} library(sf) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} library(leaflet) library(paletteer) ## read data zmovements <-read_csv("z_movements.csv") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: One or more parsing issues, call `problems()` on your data frame for details, e.g.: dat <- vroom(...) problems(dat) ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Rows: 13 Columns: 5 ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," chr (3): person, start, destination dbl (1): journey_id date (1): date ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} zlocations <-read_csv("z_locations.csv") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Rows: 2 Columns: 4 ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," chr (2): location, address dbl (2): lat, lng ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} #sf object zlocations_sf <- st_as_sf(zlocations, coords = c("lng", "lat"), crs = 4326) #joining geocoded data to attribute data and then turning it into an sf object zmovements_geo <- left_join(zmovements, zlocations, by = join_by(destination == location)) zmovements_sf <- st_as_sf(zmovements_geo, coords = c("lng", "lat"), crs = 4326) #using the group by and summarize workflow zmovements_lines <- zmovements_sf |> arrange(date) |> group_by(person) |> summarise(do_union = FALSE) |> st_cast("LINESTRING") #leaflet map pal <- colorFactor( palette = as.character(paletteer_d("colorBlindness::paletteMartin")), domain = zmovements_lines$person) leaflet() |> addTiles() |> addPolylines(data = zmovements_lines, color = ~pal(person), opacity = 0.8, label = ~person) |> addCircleMarkers(data = zlocations_sf, fillOpacity = 0.5, radius = 5, label = ~location) |> addLegend("bottomleft", pal = pal, values = zmovements_lines$person) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Map of Trips to Aunt Zerviah - One line for each individual ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} "library(sf) library(tidygeocoder) library(USAboundaries) library(tidyverse) library(leaflet) library(paletteer) zlocations <- read.csv(z_locations.csv) zmovements <- read.csv(z_movements.csv) zlocations_sf <- st_as_sf(zlocations, coords = c(lng, lat), crs = 4326) zmovements_wide <- zmovements |> arrange(date) |> group_by(person) |> ungroup() |> filter(!is.na(start)) |> rowid_to_column(journeyid) zmovements_long <- zmovements_wide |> pivot_longer( cols = start:destination, names_to = type, values_to = location ) zmovements_long_sf <- zmovements_long |> left_join(zlocations, by = location) |> st_as_sf(coords = c(lng, lat), crs = 4326) |> group_by(journey_id) |> summarise(do_union = FALSE) |> st_cast(LINESTRING) journeys_sf <- left_join(zmovements_long_sf, zmovements_wide, by = journey_id) journeys_sf journeys_sf <- journeys_sf |> mutate(label = paste ( person,
, start, to, destination,
, year(date),day(date), month(date, label = TRUE))) pal <- colorFactor( palette = as.character(paletteer_d(colorBlindness::paletteMartin)), domain = journeys_sf$person) leaflet() |> addTiles() |> addPolylines(data = journeys_sf, color = ~pal(person), opacity = 0.8, label = ~person) |> addCircleMarkers(data = zlocations_sf, fillOpacity = 0.5, radius = 5, label = ~location) |> addLegend(bottomleft, pal = pal, values = journeys_sf$person)" ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "library(sf)\nlibrary(tidygeocoder)\nlibrary(USAboundaries)\nlibrary(tidyverse)\nlibrary(leaflet)\nlibrary(paletteer)\n\nzlocations <- read.csv(z_locations.csv)\nzmovements <- read.csv(z_movements.csv)\n\nzlocations_sf <- st_as_sf(zlocations,\n coords = c(lng, lat),\n crs = 4326)\nzmovements_wide <- zmovements |> \n arrange(date) |> \n group_by(person) |>\n ungroup() |>\n filter(!is.na(start)) |>\n rowid_to_column(journeyid)\n\nzmovements_long <- zmovements_wide |> \n pivot_longer(\n cols = start:destination,\n names_to = type,\n values_to = location\n )\n\nzmovements_long_sf <- zmovements_long |> \n left_join(zlocations, by = location) |> \n st_as_sf(coords = c(lng, lat), crs = 4326) |> \n group_by(journey_id) |> \n summarise(do_union = FALSE) |> \n st_cast(LINESTRING)\n\njourneys_sf <- left_join(zmovements_long_sf, zmovements_wide, by = journey_id)\njourneys_sf\n\njourneys_sf <- journeys_sf |> \n mutate(label = paste (\n person,
,\n start, to, destination,
,\n year(date),day(date), month(date, label = TRUE)))\n\npal <- colorFactor(\n palette = as.character(paletteer_d(colorBlindness::paletteMartin)),\n domain = journeys_sf$person)\n\nleaflet() |> \n addTiles() |>\n addPolylines(data = journeys_sf,\n color = ~pal(person),\n opacity = 0.8,\n label = ~person) |> \n addCircleMarkers(data = zlocations_sf,\n fillOpacity = 0.5,\n radius = 5,\n label = ~location) |> \n addLegend(bottomleft, pal = pal, values = journeys_sf$person)" ``` ::: :::
